title: Interpret What A Disabled Charger Means In OptiSweep Charging Control
summary: This source-backed reference explains that a disabled charger in the Charger Selection view is a logical allocation state, not a hardware power-off. A disabled charger remains physically present but is marked unavailable for AGV allocation, so RMS will not route AGVs to that charging location. When the charger is enabled again, the station can be reused for AGVs in the charging queue.
when_to_use: Use this reference when reviewing charger status in the Charger Selection view and you need to correctly interpret what enabled versus disabled means for OptiSweep charging behavior and AGV allocation.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to conclude that charger hardware power has been shut off.
Do not use this runbook to infer undocumented hardware effects from the disabled state.
Do not use this runbook as a physical charger repair or electrical shutdown procedure.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review charger status in the Charger Selection view and interpret enabled or disabled state according to the documented OptiSweep charging behavior.

safety_notes: The source states that disabling a charger does not kill the charger itself; do not assume a disabled state means hardware shutdown.
Do not assume undocumented hardware behavior from the enabled or disabled state.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Charger Selection screen
Documented understanding of charger enabled and disabled meanings

access_required: Access to the Charger Selection screen
system_preconditions: The charger of interest can be located in the Charger Selection view by charger ID.

visual_references.description: Charger Selection view and training explanation of charger enabled/disabled status and disable function.

steps.title: Open Charger Selection and locate the charger
steps.purpose: Identify the specific charger whose state needs interpretation.
steps.instruction: Open the Charger Selection view and locate the charger of interest by its unique charger ID.
steps.expected_result: The target charger is visible in the Charger Selection view.
steps.healthy_condition: The charger can be identified by charger ID in the Charger Selection screen.
steps.failure_condition: The charger cannot be located or the Charger Selection view is not available.

actions.label: Open Charger Selection
actions.exact_action: Open the Charger Selection view and locate the charger of interest by charger ID.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Charger Selection screen showing charger identification by unique charger ID.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the charger of interest cannot be identified in the Charger Selection view.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The slide and aligned transcript describe selecting a charger by unique charger ID and viewing charger status.

steps.title: Check whether the charger is enabled or disabled
steps.purpose: Determine the displayed charger state before interpreting its operational meaning.
steps.instruction: Check whether the charger is shown as enabled or disabled in the Charger Selection view.
steps.expected_result: The displayed charger state is identified as enabled or disabled.
steps.healthy_condition: The charger status is clearly visible in the Charger Selection view.
steps.failure_condition: The charger status cannot be determined from the available screen information.

actions.label: Inspect charger status
actions.exact_action: Check whether the charger is shown as enabled or disabled.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Charger Selection screen showing charger status and disable function.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the charger status cannot be determined from the Charger Selection view.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The slide and aligned transcript describe viewing charger status and checking whether the charger is enabled or disabled.

steps.title: Interpret disabled as a logical allocation state
steps.purpose: Apply the documented meaning of the disabled charger state.
steps.instruction: Interpret a disabled charger state as a logical allocation control in OptiSweep charging behavior, not as a hardware shutdown of the charger itself.
steps.expected_result: The disabled state is understood as logical unavailability rather than physical power loss.
steps.healthy_condition: The charger is understood to remain physically present while being logically unavailable for allocation.
steps.failure_condition: The disabled state is misread as meaning the charger hardware has been killed or powered off.

actions.label: Interpret disabled meaning
actions.exact_action: Interpret a disabled state as a logical allocation control, not as a hardware shutdown.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training explanation that disable does not kill the charger itself.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and correct the interpretation if anyone assumes the disabled state means charger hardware shutdown.
Escalate if observed system behavior appears inconsistent with the documented meaning.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Disable does not kill the charger itself; it logically disables the charger so RMS will not route AGVs to that charging location.
Disabled charger status means the charger remains physically present but is logically unavailable for allocation.

steps.title: Interpret routing impact of a disabled charger
steps.purpose: Understand the operational effect of the disabled state on AGV assignment.
steps.instruction: Use the documented source meaning to conclude that RMS will not route AGVs to a charger that is disabled.
steps.expected_result: The routing consequence of the disabled state is correctly understood.
steps.healthy_condition: A disabled charger is treated as unavailable for AGV routing and allocation.
steps.failure_condition: A disabled charger is assumed to remain eligible for AGV routing.

actions.label: Interpret routing behavior
actions.exact_action: Verify from the source-backed meaning that RMS will not route AGVs to a charger that is disabled.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training explanation that RMS will not route AGVs to a disabled charging location.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if observed routing behavior does not match the documented statement that RMS will not route AGVs to a disabled charging location.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Disable does not kill the charger itself; it logically disables the charger so RMS will not route AGVs to that charging location.
RMS does not route AGVs to a disabled charging location.

steps.title: Interpret enabled as allowing charger reuse
steps.purpose: Apply the documented meaning of the enabled state after a charger has been disabled.
steps.instruction: When the charger is later shown as enabled again, interpret that state as allowing the station to be reused for AGVs in the charging queue.
steps.expected_result: The enabled state is understood as restoring charger availability for reuse.
steps.healthy_condition: An enabled charger is understood to be available again for queued AGVs.
steps.failure_condition: The charger is assumed to remain unavailable after it is shown as enabled again.

actions.label: Interpret enabled meaning
actions.exact_action: When the charger is later shown as enabled again, interpret that state as allowing the station to be reused for AGVs in the charging queue.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training explanation that enabling the charger allows the station to be reused.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the charger is shown as enabled but system behavior does not reflect reuse availability.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When you enable it, Geek sends a message saying you've enabled that charger and then we can reuse that station.
After recovery, the charger can be re-enabled so queued AGVs can again be assigned there.

steps.title: Record the charger state meaning accurately
steps.purpose: Ensure support communication and documentation stay aligned with the source-backed meaning.
steps.instruction: Record or communicate the charger state meaning accurately without assuming undocumented hardware behavior.
steps.expected_result: The charger state is documented or communicated using the source-backed interpretation only.
steps.healthy_condition: Notes or support communication describe disabled as logical unavailability and enabled as reuse availability, without unsupported hardware claims.
steps.failure_condition: Documentation or communication includes unsupported assumptions about hardware shutdown or other undocumented effects.

actions.label: Document supported meaning
actions.exact_action: Record the charger state meaning accurately without assuming undocumented hardware behavior.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if observed behavior does not match the documented meaning of disabled or enabled charger states.
Do not proceed with unsupported conclusions about hardware effects.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Disable does not kill the charger itself; it logically disables the charger so RMS will not route AGVs to that charging location.
When you enable it, Geek sends a message saying you've enabled that charger and then we can reuse that station.

success_criteria: The user correctly interprets a disabled charger as logically unavailable for AGV allocation rather than physically powered off.
The user correctly understands that RMS will not route AGVs to a disabled charging location.
The user correctly understands that an enabled charger can be reused for AGVs in the charging queue.
Support notes and communication avoid unsupported assumptions about charger hardware behavior.
healthy_conditions: The charger state can be identified in the Charger Selection view.
Disabled state is interpreted as logical allocation control.
Enabled state is interpreted as allowing charger reuse.
Support communication remains limited to source-backed behavior.
failure_conditions: The disabled state is interpreted as charger hardware shutdown.
A disabled charger is assumed to remain available for AGV routing.
An enabled charger is assumed to remain unavailable for reuse.
Observed system behavior does not match the documented meaning of enabled or disabled charger states.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed system behavior does not match the documented meaning of disabled or enabled charger states.
Escalate if charger status cannot be determined from the Charger Selection view.
Do not assume undocumented hardware effects from the disabled state.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Charger Selection screen and training explanation of enabled/disabled charger meaning.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Disable does not kill the charger itself; it logically disables the charger so RMS will not route AGVs to that charging location.
When you enable it, Geek sends a message saying you've enabled that charger and then we can reuse that station.
